title: Phenomenon 1984 (Joke)
wikitext:
{{M.E.G. Theme}} {{Joke}} __Notoc__ {{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 1984: '''"Literally 1984"'''}}
{{M.E.G Notice|1=Hazardous Properties|reason=By interacting with this page, you will fall victim to the effects of Phenomenon 1984.}} {{Quote|1=A warning? For this? It's like were in 1984.|2=James Shitwell}} ==Description== '''Phenomenon 1984''', also known as '''"Literally 1984"''' is a verbal trigger phrase that spreads to wanderers by sound. Upon acquisition of this virus, those affected will be compelled to use the same trigger when interacting with anything that restrains the individual in some way. The use of the term has spread to any action with a negative response on the wanderer, however it is most present in the case of limitation. How the outbreak first reached the Backrooms remains unknown, though it appears to be an issue in baseline reality as well. Its first appearance within the frontrooms dates to June 8, 1949, alongside the publication of the dystopian novel "1984" by George Orwell. ==George Orwell== {{Blurimage|file=GeorgieOrwellie.png|caption=A non-metaphysical photo of George Orwell.}} First found in the Backrooms on June 14th, 1949, George Orwell was a known reality bender and metaphysical artist. Alongside his mass release of "1984", all pieces of physical media developed by George Orwell causes unknown anomalous effects on the consumer, often in relation to the theme of the media. Upon his initial interviews with MEG and Backrooms populous, he assumed an unassuming identity, only vaguely speaking on his career and current events in the frontrooms. For most of his time in the Backrooms, George Orwell remained in [[Level 213]] as he wrote additional pieces. Since his death and discovery of the phenomenon, multiple large groups (such as [[M.E.G.]] and [[Republic of Aurielle]]) have engaged in mass book-burnings to prevent others from being affected. Additionally, the [[The AEG Procedure|AEG]] has successfully reported removing all creations by George Orwell in [[Level 906]], though this has yet to be fully verified because of the size of the level. Below is a list of known books by George Orwell and their anomalous traits. {| class="article-table" style="border:2px solid white" ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| Animal Farm ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Wanders suddenly become extremely affixed to United States politics (specifically US President Donald Trump) and will act in ways that "support his agenda" despite them not living within the country. This fascination has existed before Donald Trump's birth or political career. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| A Clergyman’s Daughter ! style="padding:10px 10px"| Beginning as a passion towards the Catholic faith, wanders will spiral downward into extreme fanaticism and will engage in terrorism. This culminates into assuming themselves to be the next virgin Mary and ████ █████ ██ ██ ██████████████ █████. Upon death via trauma to the internal organs, the █████ will consume the host's flesh and will grow to live a normal life. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| Burmese Days ! style="padding:10px 10px"| After reading, whenever wanderers hear about Myanmar, Burma, or India, they will begin reciting parts of the novel completely in Burmese, focusing on the main character's concubine and their interactions with the doctor character ''Dr. Veraswami''. These stop after roughly 1 hour or the wanderer passes out from exhaustion. |- ! style="padding:10px 10px; border-right:1px solid white"| Down and Out in Paris and London ! style="padding:10px 10px"| The digestive system (specifically the stomach) will rupture with the force of about 20 G's, killing them instantly after reading. That's it. No one can bear reading about Brit*in and Fr*nce at the same time. |} ==Properties== Upon hearing discussion of 1984, wanderers will be compelled to talk about various issues and relate them back to the novel. Often jumping through logical hoops to reach the conclusion that this experience is the exact same as the one outlined in the book. As long as a wanderer processes the trigger phrase "literally 1984", they will be under the effect of Phenomenon 1984. The only known way to get rid of '''Phenomenon 1984''' is social isolation, making them unable to spread the pathogen. This strategy may last upwards of a week, and those afflicted will often incessantly repeat the saying, attributing any though or concept to being "just like 1984". Provided is an incomplete list of things wanderers have claimed to be "literally 1984": * The lack of naturally generating bathrooms in [[Level 188]]. * Thomas Benadolli's username being changed from "RigatoniTom" to "RavioliTom" despite changed back every time. * MEG doing anything ever. * MEG doing nothing. * Forgetting the exit in [[Level 525]]. * Any political ideology that isn't Economic Conservatism. * Economic Conservatism. * The existence of non-conforming gender identities in the Backrooms. * Cascadans. * The Backrooms having no exit. * [[Temporary Manifestation|{{Delete}}]]. * Separation of anomalous levels/entities/objects from regular levels/entities/objects. * [[The Narrator]]. * Whenever the [[Movable Statues]] move. * A lack of cows in [[Level 204]]. * [[Level Fun]] not being fun. * Linda Kent's allergy to almonds * Christmas-themed levels (in July). * Getting your [[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]] spammed by cognitohazardous files because no can agree if it's "no-clipping" or "noclipping". * Your fancy military gear being destroyed by a crack addict with [[Object 10|explosive shrooms]].
